Title: A β-ketoacyl carrier protein reductase confers heat tolerance via the regulation of fatty acid biosynthesis and stress signaling in rice

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: KAR superfamily; short-chain dehydrogenase/reductase-type ketoreductase domain
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: plastid/chloroplast type II fatty acid synthase elongation cycle
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: 3-ketoacyl-ACP (assayed with acetoacetyl-CoA/NADH in vitro as KAR substrate surrogate)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces 3-ketoacyl-ACP to 3-hydroxyacyl-ACP during the type II fatty acid elongation cycle using reducing equivalents.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool; required for de novo synthesis of membrane fatty acids that support downstream glycerolipid accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Membrane biogenesis and stability; maintenance of chloroplast integrity and heat tolerance.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: leaf membranes, especially chloroplast/thylakoid-associated membrane lipids
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes de novo fatty acid accumulation and heat tolerance; the A254T mutant allele reduces KAR activity, lowers C16:0 and C18:3 fatty acids and membrane glycerolipids, and causes heat hypersensitivity.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Under normal conditions the mutant shows mild pale-green and growth defects, including slightly reduced chlorophyll, plant height, tiller number, seed-setting rate and grain size.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control enzyme required for de novo fatty acid synthesis under heat stress, but not explicitly established as the sole rate-limiting step.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: EMS mutant mapping; map-based cloning; complementation; recombinant enzyme assay; heat stability assay; subcellular localization; lipidomics; fatty acid profiling; qRT-PCR; physiological heat-stress phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Lipidomics (UHPLC-Q Exactive HF MS); no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native HTS1 promoter for complementation and pHTS1::GUS; CaMV 35S promoter for GFP localization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Predominantly expressed in green tissues, highest in leaves and leaf sheaths, followed by panicles; strongly induced by heat stress
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly expressed in green tissues, highest in leaves and leaf sheaths, followed by panicles; transcript peaks after 3 h at 45°C and protein peaks after 6 h heat treatment
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heat stress at 45°C strongly induces HTS1 and reveals metabolic phenotype; much weaker response to salt, cold and PEG drought treatments
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for breeding or engineering rice heat tolerance by maintaining fatty acid biosynthesis and membrane stability under high temperature; caution that partial loss of function causes mild growth penalties.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The causal mutation is a G-to-A substitution causing A254T and an 85.7% reduction in KAR activity. HTS1 is thermostable at 45°C. The rice genome contains two homologs, suggesting partial redundancy.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HTS1/LOC_Os04g30760 was cloned as the causal gene of the heat-sensitive hts1 mutant and complementation restored wild-type heat tolerance. 2. HTS1 encodes a chloroplast thylakoid-localized β-ketoacyl-ACP reductase with strong KAR activity, whereas the A254T mutation sharply decreases activity. 3. Loss of HTS1 function reduces total fatty acids, especially C16:0 and C18:3, lowers DG and MGDG accumulation, destabilizes membranes and chloroplasts, and impairs heat tolerance. 4. HTS1 is heat inducible and thermostable, supporting its importance in sustaining fatty acid biosynthesis during heat stress.
